justification: Variant B is the more useful operator briefing despite a questionable competitive frame (treating 3 Burger and FLOW CAFE as Monsun's peers when Monsun is Pan-Asian — a real miss). It compensates with sharper specificity: named addresses, concrete prices (46 PLN burger, 41 PLN Croque Madame, 35 PLN breakfast, 2.49M PLN listing), dated review velocity deltas, and a verifiable infrastructure narrative ("rowerowy rebus") tying foot-traffic loss to a municipal cause. Source diversity is broader (commercial real estate portals, KRS, GoWork, local food media) versus A's heavier delivery-platform lean. A's cuisine-matched competitor set (HAOS, PANDA WOK, Chinese Wok, Moon, MIWO) is more correct on framing, but its sharpest quantitative claim — the 'API proxy' rating distribution — is admittedly Monsun's own data, not competitors', which weakens its competitive intelligence value. B's takeaways (margin protection over discounting, direct-channel pivot) are more immediately actionable this week. Specialist sets are equivalent for this query.
spot_checks: A: HAOS 'salmonella' and food-poisoning chatter is plausible from Trojmiasto.pl forums but unverifiable here; the '77.8% ignored negative reviews' figure derives from Monsun's own Google Places data used as a 'proxy', which is methodologically weak. B: 3 Burger Świętojańska 61 with 3burger.pl direct channel is internally consistent with the cited drawer domain; FLOW CAFE @ Świętojańska 69 with @flowcafegdynia handle and 4.7 rating cross-references cleanly.
